command.name: Batch 3D Print
command.description: Export a sequence of 3MF, STL, or OBJ files while changing a Fusion user parameter between each export.
command.tooltip_description:
Generate a batch of parameter-driven mesh exports from one body, occurrence, or root component.

Choose a user parameter, set the starting value, increment, and file count, then export each variation as 3MF, STL, or OBJ.

This is useful for numbered tags, labels, serials, size variants, and other models where the geometry or text changes from one file to the next.

GitHub: NathanNevesPrates/Fusion360-Batch-3D-Print

prompts.geometry: Select one body, occurrence, or root component.

geometry.base: The model geometry exported for every generated file.
geometry.description: Select one body, occurrence, or root component. The add-in reuses this selection after each parameter update.

paramSelect.base: The user parameter that changes between exports.
paramSelect.description: Existing user parameters are listed here. Choose the manual option if the parameter is not shown.

paramName.base: Type the exact user parameter name.
paramName.description: Parameter names are case-sensitive. This field is only used when the manual option is selected.

paramMode.base: How the parameter receives each generated value.
paramMode.description: Number only writes values such as 1, 2, 3. Text label using format writes a string built from the Text format field.

textFormat.description: Use {n} where the current number should appear, for example: Label {n}. Only used in text mode.

startValue.description: For example, a starting value of 1 begins the sequence at 1.

increment.base: The amount added after each export.
increment.description: Use 1 for 1, 2, 3. Use 5 for 5, 10, 15. Negative values are allowed.

fileCount.description: The default is intentionally small to reduce accidental large batch exports.

exportFormat.base: The mesh file format to create.
exportFormat.description: Available formats are 3MF, STL Binary, STL ASCII, and OBJ.

unitType.base: Distance units for STL and OBJ exports.
unitType.description: This setting is disabled for 3MF because Fusion's 3MF export options do not expose the same unit field.

refinement.description: Low, Medium, and High use Fusion's preset mesh refinement settings. Custom exposes advanced fields below.

surfaceDeviation.description: Only used when Refinement is Custom. Larger values create coarser meshes; smaller values create denser meshes.

normalDeviation.description: Only used when Refinement is Custom. This controls how far mesh normals can deviate from the model surface normals.

maximumEdgeLength.base: Maximum triangle edge length in centimeters.
maximumEdgeLength.description: Only used when Refinement is Custom. Leave at 0 to avoid forcing a maximum edge length.

aspectRatio.description: Only used when Refinement is Custom. Leave at 0 to avoid forcing an aspect ratio.

oneFilePerBody.base: Export each body separately when possible.
oneFilePerBody.description: Used when exporting an occurrence or root component. Fusion appends body names when it creates multiple files.

fileBase.base: Base name used for files and the generated folder.
fileBase.description: When a selection is made, this field is filled from the selected body/component name. You can edit it before exporting.

outputParentFolder.base: Parent location for the generated batch folder.
outputParentFolder.description: Use the Choose folder button to set this path. The add-in creates a subfolder inside it.

browseFolder.description: Choose the parent folder where the generated batch export folder should be created.

outputFolderPreview.base: Preview of the folder that will be created.
outputFolderPreview.description: The folder name is generated from the File base name plus the configured suffix.

notes.description: Edit lang/en-us.json to change this text for your own release.

notes.command:
Workflow: set parameter -> recompute design -> export mesh -> repeat.
The selected parameter is restored after the batch unless disabled in config.json.
Preview and triangle count are intentionally not shown because the model changes during the batch.
This command always exports files; it does not send every generated mesh to a print utility.

messages.no_design: Open a Fusion design before running Batch 3D Print.
messages.parameter_missing: No user parameter named "{name}" was found. Parameter names are case-sensitive.
messages.large_batch_warning: You are about to export {count} files. The warning threshold is {threshold}. Continue?
